title: Fix crash when handling empty adb key list
body: This commit fixes a crash in the ADB module when no keys were parsed. We should return an empty list by default.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -120,7 +120,7 @@ def parse(self, content: bytes) -> None:
 
         # Calculate key fingerprints for better readability
         key_info = []
-        for user_key in parsed.get("user_keys"):
+        for user_key in parsed.get("user_keys", []):
             user_info = self.calculate_key_info(user_key)
             key_info.append(user_info)

steps.operation: Harden parsing logic by defaulting missing/empty ADB key lists to an empty iterable when generating key fingerprints
steps.reason: `parsed.get("user_keys")` can return `None` when no keys are present, causing a crash when iterated; providing `[]` ensures the loop is safe and results in an empty `key_info` list as intended.
steps.side_effects: Prevents exceptions in ADB artifact parsing when `user_keys` is absent/empty; downstream consumers will now consistently receive an empty list rather than an error, which may change behavior for any code/tests that previously expected a failure in this scenario.

step_plan.summary: Fixes a crash in the ADB dumpsys parser by safely iterating over an empty default when no `user_keys` are parsed, returning an empty key info list instead of raising an exception.
